seo_description: Current time in Fiji. Live FJT clock — UTC+12 standard, UTC+13 daylight saving (November to January). Fiji time vs New York, London, Sydney, Auckland.

faq.question: What time is it in Fiji right now?
faq.answer: Fiji currently operates on Fiji Time (FJT/UTC+12) during standard time from February through October, and Fiji Summer Time (FJST/UTC+13) during daylight saving from November through January. Suva, the capital, is one of the first major cities to welcome each new day due to its position near the International Date Line. Use the live clock above for the exact current time in Fiji.

faq.question: What time zone is Fiji in?
faq.answer: Fiji uses Fiji Time (FJT), which is UTC+12 during standard time and UTC+13 during Daylight Saving Time. Fiji is located in the South Pacific Ocean, just west of the International Date Line, placing it among the world's easternmost time zones. At UTC+12, Fiji is 12 hours ahead of UTC, 17 hours ahead of New York (EST), and 2 hours ahead of Sydney during standard periods.

faq.question: Does Fiji observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: Yes. Fiji observes Daylight Saving Time (DST) during the Southern Hemisphere summer. Clocks advance one hour from the last Sunday in October at 2:00 AM to the third Sunday in January at 3:00 AM. During this DST period, Fiji operates at UTC+13, making it one of the few places in the world to regularly use a UTC+13 offset. This aligns Fiji more closely with New Zealand during summer months.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Fiji and New York?
faq.answer: Fiji is 17 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5), and 16 hours ahead during Eastern Daylight Time (EDT/UTC-4). When DST is also active in Fiji, the offset shifts further. When it is 9:00 AM Monday in New York (EST), it is 2:00 AM Tuesday in Fiji (FJT). The combination of both regions observing DST at different times of year makes the exact gap variable — check the live converter for precise differences.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Fiji and London?
faq.answer: Fiji is 12 hours ahead of London during GMT (UTC+0) in winter, and 11 hours ahead during British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1). When Fiji is also on FJST (UTC+13), it is 13 hours ahead of London in winter. When it is 8:00 AM in London (GMT), it is 8:00 PM the same day in Suva (FJT). Despite the large offset, this creates a convenient early-evening window in Fiji that aligns with London morning hours.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Fiji and Sydney?
faq.answer: Fiji is typically 2 hours ahead of Sydney during Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST/UTC+10), and the gap may narrow to 1 hour when Australia observes daylight saving (AEDT/UTC+11). During Fiji's DST (FJST/UTC+13), Fiji and Sydney (AEDT/UTC+11) are just 2 hours apart. Sydney and Suva are the two closest major hubs in the region, and scheduling between them is relatively straightforward.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Suva, Fiji?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Suva run 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM FJT Monday through Friday, with a one-hour lunch break at noon. Government offices typically close at 4:30 PM. Banks operate 9:30 AM to 3:00 PM Monday through Thursday and 9:30 AM to 4:00 PM on Fridays. Retail shops in Suva's CBD are open 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM on weekdays and 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM on Saturdays. The tourism sector operates across extended hours, especially at resort islands.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Fiji from Australia or New Zealand?
faq.answer: From Sydney (AEST/UTC+10), Fiji is about 2 hours ahead — call between 6:00 AM and 3:00 PM Sydney time to reach Suva offices from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. From Auckland (NZST/UTC+12 or NZDT/UTC+13), Fiji is at a similar or identical offset during standard time, making calls from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Auckland time perfectly aligned. For callers from the US West Coast, Fiji is roughly 20 hours ahead — asynchronous communication is typically more practical.

content_blocks.title: Fiji Time Zone Guide — FJT (UTC+12) and FJST (UTC+13)
content_blocks.content: Fiji, an archipelago of over 330 islands in the South Pacific, sits just west of the International Date Line at approximately 18°S, 178°E. This position places Fiji among the world's first inhabited territories to begin each new calendar day. Fiji Standard Time (FJT) is UTC+12, while Fiji Summer Time (FJST) advances to UTC+13 during the Southern Hemisphere summer from late October to mid-January. At UTC+12, Suva is 12 hours ahead of London (GMT), 17 hours ahead of New York (EST), 20 hours ahead of Los Angeles (PST), and 2 hours ahead of Sydney (AEST). The IANA timezone identifier for Fiji is Pacific/Fiji. Because Fiji lies close to the dateline, travelers arriving from Hawaii or the US West Coast typically gain an entire calendar day, while those arriving from New Zealand or Australia experience minimal time adjustment. The relatively small spread of the Fijian archipelago means all islands share the single FJT/FJST timezone, simplifying domestic travel and communications across the island groups.

content_blocks.title: Tourism and Remote Work in Fiji — Timezone Considerations
content_blocks.content: Fiji is one of the world's premier dive and resort destinations, drawing visitors from Australia, New Zealand, the United States, and Europe. For Australian and New Zealand travelers, Fiji's timezone (FJT/UTC+12) is just 2 hours ahead of AEST and essentially identical to NZST (UTC+12), making it the most jet-lag-friendly international destination from the Oceania region. American visitors from the East Coast face a 17-hour difference, meaning arrival planning is critical — direct flights from Los Angeles typically cross the dateline and arrive in Fiji having skipped a calendar day. For remote workers considering Fiji as a base, the UTC+12 offset offers strong overlap with Australian and New Zealand business hours but requires very early mornings or late nights for real-time collaboration with European or US teams. Fiji's improving digital infrastructure, with undersea fiber cable connectivity, supports growing interest from the digital nomad community. The country's favorable tax environment and straightforward visa conditions for short stays further attract location-independent professionals from across the Pacific.

content_blocks.title: Calling Fiji Internationally — Best Windows by Region
content_blocks.content: Coordinating calls with Suva or Nadi requires navigating one of the largest UTC offsets in the Pacific. From Sydney (AEST/UTC+10), Fiji is 2 hours ahead — call between 6:00 AM and 3:00 PM Sydney time to reach local offices during the 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM FJT window. Auckland callers (NZST/UTC+12) share the same offset for much of the year, making scheduling trivial. From London (GMT/UTC+0), Fiji's 12-hour lead means reaching a Suva office (8:00 AM to 5:00 PM FJT) requires calling between 8:00 PM and 5:00 AM the previous evening — highly impractical for real-time calls. Email and asynchronous tools are strongly recommended for Europe-to-Fiji communications. New York callers (EST/UTC-5) face a 17-hour gap: business hours in Suva (8:00 AM to 5:00 PM FJT) correspond to 3:00 PM to midnight in New York the previous day. Los Angeles callers (PST/UTC-8) experience a 20-hour difference. Fiji's international dialing code is +679, and there are no area codes — local numbers are 7 digits.

timezone_facts.dst_start: Last Sunday in October at 2:00 AM
timezone_facts.dst_end: Third Sunday in January at 3:00 AM
